agents.name: retail_market_sentiment_agent.py
agents.desc: 27-gate deterioration detection. Pulse also fulfills sector-screener sentiment requests via _handle_screening_requests — same detect_cascade() per-ticker computation is dual-written to two tables intentionally: (a) sector_screening.sentiment_score for per-ticker screener display + composite-score aggregation (covers every screener candidate), (b) signals.sentiment_score via stamp_signals_sentiment() for per-signal trader Gate-5 consumption (only stamps tickers with an active QUEUED signal). agents.name: retail_sector_screener.py
agents.desc: Sector momentum scoring — 11 S&P SPDR sector ETFs × top-10 holdings each, per-ticker calc_momentum_score (3m return 50% + 20d/50d SMA 30% + 10d/30d volume 20%). Runs 2x/day (pre-market 09:15 + close 16:00) — momentum is a multi-week signal so twice daily captures yesterday's close + today's close, plenty for the slow-moving inputs. Output populates sector_screening table including momentum_score column (2026-04-21+) and ret_3m raw column (2026-04-27+, fed back from calc_momentum_score for portal display). combined_score formula re-weighted 2026-04-27 to news 30% + sentiment 30% + momentum 30% + etf_weight 10% (was 40/40/0/20) — momentum was being computed but not actually included in candidate ranking. 30-hour fault-detection stale threshold tolerates a missed day.

data_flow.trading_pipeline: Market Daemon (9:15-16:00) orchestrates enrichment → candidate_generator emits sector-driven candidates by momentum_score → validator_stack promotes to VALIDATED + writes _VALIDATOR_VERDICT ∈ {GO, CAUTION, NO_GO} and _MARKET_STATE_SCORE (sentiment 40% + news 25% + macro 35%) to customer_settings → trade_daemon (continuous 9:30-16:00, 30s cycles) pulls VALIDATED signals directly + live_prices → 13-gate eval per signal (Gate 1 halts on NO_GO verdict; Gate 5 composite now blends _MARKET_STATE_SCORE as regime nudge; Gate 6 chase caps block peak entries) → BUY / SKIP / WATCH / EXIT → Alpaca market order → _resolve_fill_price polls filled_avg_price → open_position with real entry price.
data_flow.shared_data: price_poller (standalone 24/7 timer, every 60s) → live_prices. News/Sentiment → shared signals pool → all customers see same intel. Sector screener → momentum_score on sector_screening table → candidate_generator filter.
data_flow.monitoring: Auditor → SSH scans pi4b + pi5 → company.db suggestions. Sentinel ← heartbeats → silence alerts → Scoop. Librarian → SSH package audit → CVE alerts. pi2w nodes currently disabled (power-source relocation pending).
data_flow.backup_chain: v2 (2026-05-03): pi5 retail_backup.py (1:30am) builds 3-stream split (customer + retail) → Fernet encrypt-on-source with BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY → manifest.json embedded at tar root → round-trip self-verify → POST .enc to pi4b /receive_backup → ~/.backup_staging/<stream>/<pi_id>/ → strongbox (2am) → adds company stream locally → uploads all 3 streams to Cloudflare R2 at <stream>/<pi_id>/<date>/ → post-upload validation (decrypt, manifest verify, content sha256) → 30-day retention. Restore via retail_restore.py (--source via-r2|via-company|file:) or strongbox.py --restore <stream> <pi_id>. See documentation/specs/BACKUP_SYSTEM.md.

Critical: 4-day trader halt loop diagnosed + fixed (2026-04-28): operator reported 'screener signals in the 80s but nothing has traded all day.' Diagnosis revealed a self-reinforcing loop that had been silently halting every Gate 1 evaluation since 2026-04-24. Chain: (a) Phase C trader refactor on 2026-04-20 split run() into helpers; gate1 halts now sys.exit'd from _init_clients BEFORE the orchestrator could call _send_session_summary (which writes heartbeat + AGENT_COMPLETE). (b) Pipeline-audit Gap 1 on 2026-04-24 wired Gate 1 to consume _VALIDATOR_VERDICT and halt new entries on NO_GO — the upgrade that made the missing heartbeat actually matter. (c) 2026-04-27 architectural change moved fault_detection's _master_db() from owner-customer-DB to the new shared user/signals.db, but the trader has ALWAYS written its heartbeat and AGENT_COMPLETE per-customer (one row per customer DB). After 2026-04-27, gate1_agent_liveness + gate7_schedule_compliance queried the shared DB and always found zero trader activity → STALE_HEARTBEAT_TRADE_LOGIC + NO_RUN_TRADE_LOGIC fired CRITICAL → validator emitted NO_GO + [NO_NEW_POSITIONS, SYSTEM_CRITICAL] → trader's Gate 1 halted → trader sys.exit'd → no fresh AGENT_COMPLETE → next fault scan saw stale state → loop reinforced. Trader couldn't recover on its own. Symptom: 113 VALIDATED signals + 33 EVALUATED today but only 1 ACTED_ON. Worse, gate10 stop-loss exits also blocked since Gate 1 halts the entire cycle. Two-part fix: (1) trade_logic_agent now writes log_heartbeat('OK_HALTED') + log_event('AGENT_COMPLETE', details='halted_at_gate1') BEFORE sys.exit on gate1 halt — fault detector sees the trader ran even when halted. (2) fault_detection_agent now aggregates heartbeats and AGENT_COMPLETE for per-customer agents (currently just trade_logic_agent) across ALL active customer DBs via new _scan_per_customer_for_event helper. PER_CUSTOMER_AGENTS frozenset gates the special-case path; system-wide agents (news/sentiment/screener/price_poller/interrogation) keep the existing shared-DB query. Verification post-fix: fault scan flipped from 2 critical to 0 critical (trader '78 run(s) today'); validator verdict NO_GO → CAUTION; within 90 seconds 3 new positions opened (CLF×2 + OPEN) and 8 pent-up stop-loss exits fired (4 AVGO + 4 AMD, the largest +$652.88 on master). The fact that stop-losses fired immediately confirms Gate 1 halt was blocking ALL trade activity, not just new entries. Gate 4 LIQUIDITY skips for low-volume tickers (GLXY, TXN, BE) confirm the post-Gate-1 path is now exercised normally.

Trader Alpaca-quotes 404 fix + auditor auto-resolve (2026-04-28): operator reported '40+ auditor issues being reported' and (incorrectly) 'auditor cannot connect to retail pi'. Auditor was actually connected fine (Remote scan pi5: 30 logs every 5 min throughout the day). Real diagnosis: (1) trader.get_latest_quote() routed through self._request which prepends self.base_url=paper-api.alpaca.markets; that's the trading-API host, not market-data. /v2/stocks/<T>/quotes/latest doesn't exist there → 404. Got caught when 6 customers logged ERRORs across NVDA/MU/SCHD/PCG on 09:30 ET. Fix: get_latest_quote now mirrors get_bars — direct requests.get against ALPACA_DATA_URL with circuit-breaker integration + log_api_call telemetry + (3,10) timeouts + iex feed param. Pre-fix the call ALWAYS returned (None,None,None) so quote-driven gates (Gate 5 spread, Gate 6 chase cap, mid-cycle stop tightening) were silently degrading through stale candidate['price'] / live_prices fallbacks. Trades didn't crash, just ran on cache instead of the actual tape. (2) Auditor never auto-cleared resolved conditions — transient NEGATIVE_CASH blip from a settlement-lag race self-corrected in minutes but the flag sat indefinitely. Fixed via new _auto_resolve_stale_issues sweep on company_auditor with two policies: customer_db::* conditions cleared (last_seen older than current scan_start = condition no longer fires → resolve, catches NEGATIVE_CASH directly) + generic 24h log-scan stale rule (anything not firing for 24h → resolve, fresh occurrences re-INSERT via the existing dedup WHERE-resolved=0 clause). Auditor restart immediately auto-resolved 27 stale issues (Apr 22-25-27); manual one-shot bulk-ack cleared the remaining 14 (today's trader 404 cluster + correlated noise). 41 → 0 open issues. Cosmetic log-line follow-up: 'Scan found N new issue(s)' was being inflated by the auto_resolved count; fixed to compute total_new before attaching auto_resolved.

Pi4b auditor deep-dive — 16 unresolved findings driven to 0 (2026-05-02): operator session went through every remaining auditor.db finding after yesterday's noise-floor work cleared the bottom 88%. 13 of the 16 were stale (pre-fix occurrences from yesterday's d88a744d 401 storm + duplicate-column-name + Resend latin-1 + ACCESS_REQUEST_SUBMITTED requeue, all resolved by yesterday's commits and now bulk-marked resolved on auditor.db). 3 were genuinely new bugs found today: (1) `exit_performance.exit_timestamp` NOT NULL constraint failed — schema declares the column NOT NULL but the INSERT statement at retail_database.record_exit_performance was missing it; the close transaction rolled back every time a position closed (2 hits on 2026-05-01 09:51 ET, customers 30eff008 + f313a3d9). Fix: included exit_timestamp in the INSERT (same value as created_at, both refer to the same close event). (2) Cascading SERVICE_DOWN findings when SSH itself failed — company_auditor.check_remote_health iterates per-process and per-service via _ssh_run; when SSH timed out, every check returned ok=False and emitted its own SERVICE_DOWN/PROCESS_DOWN finding even though the actual issue was just that the node was unreachable. Fix: pre-flight `ssh host true` ping at the top of check_remote_health; if the ping fails (with the new 2-retry policy), early-return empty issues[] — scan_remote_logs's NODE_UNREACHABLE finding is the single source of truth for connectivity. (3) Boot-window race producing spurious NODE_UNREACHABLE — auditor's 5-min cron ran at 04:00:30 ET while pi5 was still booting (boot completed 04:01:33), single SSH attempt timed out. Fix: _ssh_run gains 2-retry policy with 3s delay between attempts (worst-case +6s on failure paths only). All 16 stale findings resolved on auditor.db; auditor service restarted clean. Commits 3b3c447 (synthos), 32924af (synthos-company).

Pi5 audit-page deeper triage — 422 root cause + boot timing + ghost noise (2026-05-02 morning): day-2 of the audit-page deep-dive surfaced three real bugs once yesterday's noise floor was cleared. (1) The 422 storm on POST /v2/orders had 23 hits across 6 customers, all on the immediate-after-buy trailing_stop submission. Pattern analysis showed every failing buy was a fractional position (0.014 NVDA, 20.2402 UBER, 116.6294 BMNR, 0.7011 SPY, 6.5663 PTON, …) — Alpaca paper API rejects trailing_stop orders on fractional positions, a documented limitation. Fix: new _submit_trail_stop_if_whole() helper checks abs(qty - round(qty)) < 1e-9 before submitting; if fractional, log INFO + skip. gate10's internal trail-stop ratchet already manages exits for fractional positions, so the Alpaca-side stop was a belt-and-suspenders net that didn't work for fractional anyway. Helper replaces 3 identical sites (rotation entry, managed approval exec, signal-evaluation entry). Bonus: AlpacaClient._request now captures the response body (first 500 chars) on 4xx errors and includes it in the failure log line — generic 'Client Error: Unprocessable Entity' was opaque, going forward any 422/400/403/409 shows Alpaca's actual rejection reason inline. Also short-circuits the retry loop on 422 (same as 401). (2) Boot Watchdog/Portal 'inactive' false-positive returned despite Round 4 fix — today's 04:01 boot.log showed both services hitting the 15s settle window timeout. The fix code was correct; the timeout was too short for pi5's cold-boot path (network + DB init + WAL recovery genuinely takes 30-60s). Bumped to 120s — boot is one-shot, the wait is bounded. (3) GHOST positions at 04:00 boot health-check — owner DB lists positions in tickers no longer in Alpaca; trader's gate0 reconciliation auto-cleans these on the next market-open cycle but the 04:00 ET boot health-check runs ~5h before market opens. Scoop notification pipeline rebuilt (2026-05-01): user noticed scoop service was 'active' but silent for 3 days post-revival. Investigation found three structural bugs preventing every admin alert from reaching email since at least 2026-04-28. Bug 1 (schema mismatch): strongbox._alert_scoop wrote {delivered:false} to scoop_trigger.json but scoop's drain looked for {status:pending|retry}; sentinel/vault used the right schema, strongbox didn't, and strongbox events never drained. Bug 2 (one-shot drain): drain_legacy_trigger_file set _legacy_drained=True after first call so anything written after scoop boot was silently lost; combined with all three writers using only the trigger file (never scoop_queue directly), this is why scoop_queue had ZERO new events in 3 days. Bug 3 (dead-end portal auth): dispatch_portal_notification posted X-Service-Token to /api/notifications/send but that endpoint requires admin SESSION auth and ignores the header — setting PORTAL_TOKEN would have been a no-op. Fix: new agents/_shared_scoop.py module with single enqueue_scoop_event() helper that writes directly to scoop_queue with UPPERCASE 'PENDING' status; event_type → priority map (P0 emergency / P1 important / P2 transactional / P3 background); defensive fallback to trigger file on DB failure. strongbox._alert_scoop and sentinel._trigger_scoop both refactored to call the shared helper. vault._trigger_scoop deleted (zero call sites — dead code). scoop.drain_legacy_trigger_file rewritten as re-pollable (every cycle, not one-shot), accepts both delivered:false AND status:pending|retry schemas, archives processed entries to data/scoop_trigger_processed/<UTC>.json instead of leaving stuck in active file. dispatch_portal_notification + dispatch_broadcast short-circuit when PORTAL_TOKEN missing (still missing — adding service-token auth to retail_portal /api/notifications/{send,broadcast} is queued separately for after-hours work in synthos repo). scoop now logs portal-disabled state ONCE at startup instead of warning per event. db_get_next_event uses (status or '').upper() for case-insensitive RETRY check, matching db_helpers' lowercase writes. Triple-check: import sanity (5 modules clean), live writer smokes (strongbox + sentinel each produced a scoop_queue row with correct priority/audience/source/subject), end-to-end dispatch (3 real emails delivered to operator), legacy drain (delivered:false entry migrated and archived), existing tooling unchanged (--status, --test, /api/queue admin endpoints). Commit 5e8cb78 synthos-company.

Trader-visibility audit + screener composite repair (2026-04-27): user asked to verify the trader actually consumes everything that's been wired into the screener/sentiment/news chain. Audit surfaced two defects + one visibility gap. (1) sector_screener.combined_score formula was 40% news + 40% sentiment + 0% momentum + 20% ETF weight — momentum was computed per ticker but never reached the ranking the candidate generator filtered on, so a high-momentum/low-news ticker like CAT could be invisible in 'Top by composite' even though it surfaced in 'Top by momentum'. Re-weighted to 30/30/30/10 so all three inputs contribute equally. (2) sector_screening table persisted momentum_score but not the raw 3-month return that drove it; portal screener page had no way to show 'past few months performance' for each candidate. Added ret_3m column via lazy ALTER, calc_momentum_score now returns (score, ret_3m, reasoning), run_single threads ret_3m into the candidate dict, write_screening_run persists it. Portal MOMENTUM column now shows ret_3m headline + composite bar; planning drawer shows the same. After-the-fact trade audit can now verify exactly what the trader saw at decision time.
